taxonomy: Hyalis aulica is a species of plant in the family Poaceae. It is a type of grass that belongs to the tribe Andropogoneae. Further classification places it within the subfamily Panicoideae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other grass species. It is a part of the larger classification system of the plant kingdom.
uses: Hyalis aulica, also known as the alpine willowherb, has been utilized in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. Its leaves, stems, and flowers are used in various remedies to treat a range of ailments. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. Additionally, it is thought to have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, making it useful for wound care and skin infections. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
